name: Cram
author: Geoffrey Mott-Smith; popularised by Martin Gardner / AGP
description: Cram (the impartial domino-covering game, a.k.a. plugg; popularised by Martin Gardner) is like Domineering except BOTH players may place a domino in EITHER orientation (horizontal or vertical). Players alternately place one domino on two empty orthogonally-adjacent cells of a rectangular grid (default 6x6). No captures; pieces never move. Normal play: the player who cannot place a domino loses (last to place wins). The same move set is available to both players, so the game is impartial; by a mirror-strategy argument the second player wins whenever both board dimensions are even (other boards have no simple closed-form winner — e.g. 3x3 is also a second-player win).
